name: Kinetic Grand Championship
venue_name: Arcata Plaza to Ferndale Main Street, Humboldt County
description: The Kinetic Grand Championship is the World Championship kinetic sculpture race — a 42-mile, 3-day human-powered all-terrain amphibious art race held every Memorial Day weekend in Humboldt County, CA. Day 1: Arcata Plaza (start at noon) to Halvorsen Park Eureka. Day 2: Bay crossing at Samoa Bridge. Day 3: Finish line on Main Street Ferndale. Sculptures judged on Art, Engineering, Pageantry, and Speed. 2026 race: May 23-25. 2027 race: May 29-31. Reigning 2026 champions: "The Rebel Appliance." Founded 1969 by sculptor Hobart Brown in Ferndale. Organized by Kinetic Universe, a non-profit. PO Box 425, Eureka, CA 95501.
